instruction: Clarify the role of the RCU in the described fix.
input: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: netfilter: nf_tables: adapt set backend to use GC transaction API Use the GC transaction API to replace the old and buggy gc API and the busy mark approach. No set elements are removed from async garbage collection anymore, instead the _DEAD bit is set on so the set element is not visible from lookup path anymore. Async GC enqueues transaction work that might be aborted and retried later. rbtree and pipapo set backends does not set on the _DEAD bit from the sync GC path since this runs in control plane path where mutex is held. In this case, set elements are deactivated, removed and then released via RCU callback, sync GC never fails.
output: The Read-Copy-Update (RCU) mechanism plays a critical role in the fix by ensuring that set elements are safely deactivated, removed, and released without disrupting concurrent operations. The RCU callback allows for safe memory reclamation without affecting the synchronization and consistency of the control plane operations.
